title: Leaf Switch Resource Utilization
exclusions: Pure controller-only dashboards without switch-local NX-API or eAPI (UC-18.3.12 / 18.3.3 territory); Meraki or campus-only access switches lacking VXLAN/EVPN fabric roles; silent ASIC drops that bypass CPU/TCAM counters until line-rate policers trip (pair with dataplane drop UCs); wholesale NFV soft-switches where hypervisor vCPU metrics replace **show system resources** semantics.
dataSources: `index=dc_fabric` / `index=network_ops` / `index=network` (rename per governance) — five sourcetypes: **`nxos:system_resources`** (**NX-API** JSON from **`show system resources`**: **kernel/user** CPU, **memory** totals); **`nxos:hardware_capacity`** (**`show hardware capacity forwarding`**, **`show hardware capacity module`** slices → **fib_used**, **fib_max**, **tcam_used**, **tcam_max**); **`network:switch_health`** (normalized NMS/agent **cpu_pct**, **mem_used_pct**, **health_status**, **hostname**); **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** (**TCAM/FIB/L2RIB** pressure mnemonics); **`network:bgp_neighbor`** (**EVPN** peer **bgp_state**, prefix counts for churn context). Land collectors with **1546** REST/eAPI, align samples to **7777** reference layouts, enrich ACI-attached leaves via **4022** where applicable, and use **4856**/**1810** only for correlated compute motion.
app: **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)); **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)); **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)); **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)); **Splunk Add-on for VMware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)).
spl:
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(
  sourcetype="nxos:system_resources" OR sourcetype="nxos:hardware_capacity" OR
  sourcetype="network:switch_health" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR
  sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor"
)
earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval leaf=trim(lower(coalesce(hostname,host,device_name,nms_hostname,switch_id,"")))
| eval cpu_pct=tonumber(coalesce(cpu_pct,cpu_utilization,kernel_percent,cpumem_usage_cpu,""))
| eval mem_used_pct=tonumber(coalesce(mem_used_pct,mem_usage_pct,memory_usage,heap_used_pct,chassis_mem_pct,""))
| eval fib_used=tonumber(coalesce(fib_used,fib_entries_used,ipv4_routes_used,forwarding_entries_used,l3_route_entries,""))
| eval fib_max=tonumber(coalesce(fib_max,fib_entries_max,route_capacity_max,forwarding_entries_max,fib_total_entries,""))
| eval tcam_used=tonumber(coalesce(tcam_used,tcam_entries_used,hardware_tcam_used,""))
| eval tcam_max=tonumber(coalesce(tcam_max,tcam_entries_max,tcam_budget_total,""))
| eval tcam_pct_direct=tonumber(coalesce(tcam_util_pct,acl_tcam_used_pct,""))
| eval fib_util=if(isnotnull(fib_used) AND isnotnull(fib_max) AND fib_max>0, round(100*fib_used/fib_max,2), null())
| eval tcam_util=if(isnotnull(tcam_pct_direct), tcam_pct_direct, if(isnotnull(tcam_used) AND isnotnull(tcam_max) AND tcam_max>0, round(100*tcam_used/tcam_max,2), null()))
| eval syslog_res=if(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(tcam|fib|forwarding.?table|hw.?rate.?limit|low.?memory|out.?of.?memory|l2rib.*fail)"),1,0)
| eval bgp_state_l=lower(coalesce(bgp_state,state,""))
| eval bgp_churn=if(sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor" AND match(bgp_state_l,"(idle|active|connect|opensent|openconfirm)"),1,0)
| eval cpu_b=if(isnotnull(cpu_pct) AND cpu_pct>=85,1,0)
| eval mem_b=if(isnotnull(mem_used_pct) AND mem_used_pct>=88,1,0)
| eval fib_b=if(isnotnull(fib_util) AND fib_util>=90,1,0)
| eval tcam_b=if(isnotnull(tcam_util) AND tcam_util>=85,1,0)
| eval health_sig=case(
    sourcetype="network:switch_health" AND match(lower(coalesce(health_status,status,resource_state,"")),"critical|major|down|failed"),4,
    true(),0)
| fillnull value=0 syslog_res bgp_churn cpu_b mem_b fib_b tcam_b health_sig
| eval score=cpu_b*3 + mem_b*3 + fib_b*5 + tcam_b*5 + syslog_res*4 + bgp_churn*2 + health_sig
| where score>=3 OR fib_b==1 OR tcam_b==1 OR health_sig>=4
| bin _time span=10m
| stats max(cpu_pct) AS cpu_peak max(mem_used_pct) AS mem_peak max(fib_util) AS fib_util_peak max(tcam_util) AS tcam_util_peak max(score) AS score_peak values(sourcetype) AS st_seen latest(fib_used) AS fib_used_latest latest(fib_max) AS fib_cap latest(tcam_used) AS tcam_used_latest latest(tcam_max) AS tcam_cap BY _time leaf
| eval story=leaf."|cpu=".tostring(round(coalesce(cpu_peak,0)))."|mem=".tostring(round(coalesce(mem_peak,0)))."|fib=".tostring(round(coalesce(fib_util_peak,0)))."|tcam=".tostring(round(coalesce(tcam_util_peak,0)))
| sort - score_peak - _time
| head 200
| table _time leaf cpu_peak mem_peak fib_util_peak tcam_util_peak fib_used_latest fib_cap tcam_used_latest tcam_cap score_peak st_seen story
description: Correlates NX-OS system resource polls, hardware forwarding-capacity exports, normalized third-party switch health scores, NX-OS syslog lines that name TCAM/FIB/L2RIB pressure, and EVPN BGP neighbor posture—surfacing **cpu_pct**, **mem_used_pct**, **fib_used/fib_max**, **tcam_used/tcam_max**, and **hostname** so operators catch leaf control-plane starvation and table exhaustion across Cisco Nexus and Arista EOS VXLAN/EVPN fabrics.
value: When leaves run out of headroom, EVPN updates slip, MAC learning pauses, and hardware tables edge toward line-rate blackholes long before basic ping tests fail. Surfacing utilization early gives netops and capacity teams time to add devices, trim unnecessary routes or ACLs, and sequence upgrades before tenant traffic or change windows collapse into bridge calls.
implementation: Land the five sourcetypes on governed indexes; normalize **hostname**; publish **vendor_capacity_map.csv** and **fabric_leaf_inventory.csv**; **FIELDALIAS** legacy poll field names; schedule **uc_18_3_17_leaf_resource_core** and clone **uc_18_3_17_leaf_resource_alert** with suppress windows tied to CMDB maintenance.
visualization: (1) Heatmap — leaf × (**cpu_peak**, **mem_peak**, **fib_util_peak**, **tcam_util_peak**) z-scored per pod. (2) Line/area — seven-day trends with change overlays. (3) Single-value — site **max(score_peak)**. (4) Table — **story**, caps, **st_seen** for ITSM. (5) Histogram — **score_peak** distribution for quarterly capacity decks.
schema: CIM Performance hosts time-series CPU/memory style metrics; Inventory carries **hostname**, role, and platform tags; Alerts mirrors severity-style **health_status** and syslog-derived fault text once normalized.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance only after **fib_util**/**tcam_util** extractions stabilize; keep syslog lanes unaccelerated until LINE_BREAKER proves stable for multi-line TCAM traps.

references.title: Cisco Nexus 9000 Series NX-OS System Management Configuration Guide, Release 10.3.x
Cisco Nexus 9000 Series NX-OS Verified Scalability Guide
Arista EOS User Manual — system health and platform command reference
Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform (Splunkbase 7777)

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Index **dc_fabric**, **network_ops**, or **network** per governance; isolate lab traffic so capacity math is not polluted. Run **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) on Heavy Forwarders with TLS to NX-API/eAPI; store secrets in **passwords.conf**, not savedsearches.
• Use **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) layouts as panel templates only—rebind macros to your indexes and sourcetypes.
• On **APIC-attached** fabrics, **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) complements but does not replace **CLI** **FIB/TCAM** tables—MO faults can lag **show hardware capacity forwarding** during EVPN churn.
• Map **Arista EOS** **show system resources** and platform forwarding-capacity output into the same **cpu_pct**, **mem_used_pct**, **fib_used**, **fib_max**, **tcam_used**, **tcam_max**, **hostname** fields via **1546** or an automation gateway.
• **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) and **Splunk Add-on for VMware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) add vMotion context when TOR CPU spikes correlate to **VTEP** churn—do not treat them as switch telemetry substitutes.
• Maintain **fabric_leaf_inventory.csv** with canonical **hostname**, **pod**, **vendor**, **suppress_until** for approved rebuilds. Service accounts need read-only rights for **show system resources**, **show hardware capacity forwarding**, and **show bgp l2vpn evpn summary** (no configure tokens).
• Strip operator EXEC usernames from syslog where policy requires; keep mnemonics for TCAM/FIB/resource faults.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• **nxos:system_resources** — NX-API **ins_api** to **show system resources** every **300s** (60–120s during migrations). Emit **cpu_pct** as worst-case supervisor busy% and **mem_used_pct** from total memory counters; if only bytes print, compute used/max*100 in transforms.
• **nxos:hardware_capacity** — Poll **show hardware capacity forwarding** (and module-scoped commands when linecards partition FIB). Normalize route/host/MAC/LPM banks into **fib_used** and **fib_max**; record table names in **capacity_table** for drilldowns.
• **network:switch_health** — Fold NMS/agent JSON into one sourcetype with **health_status**, **cpu_pct**, **mem_used_pct**, **hostname**; dedupe multi-poller echoes via collector_id.
• **cisco:nxos:syslog** — Forward RLIMIT, TCAM, FIB, L2RIB, and critical ACL messages via SC4S or UF; align **TIME_FORMAT** to device time zone.
• **network:bgp_neighbor** — Track EVPN neighbor FSM, last flap, and prefixes per leaf; include **bgp_state** and **neighbor** for churn math with **cpu_pct**.
• **EOS guardrail** — Keep **vendor_capacity_map.csv** translating Arista CLI bank labels into **tcam_used/tcam_max** so panels compare fairly with Cisco rows.
• Expect one compact JSON event per leaf per poll plus sparse syslog; target <5 MB/day/leaf at five-minute cadence with compression on the wire.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save **uc_18_3_17_leaf_resource_core**, clone **uc_18_3_17_leaf_resource_alert** for paging, and wrap indexes in **macro_dc_fabric_indexes**.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=network)
(
  sourcetype="nxos:system_resources" OR sourcetype="nxos:hardware_capacity" OR
  sourcetype="network:switch_health" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR
  sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor"
)
earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval leaf=trim(lower(coalesce(hostname,host,device_name,nms_hostname,switch_id,"")))
| eval cpu_pct=tonumber(coalesce(cpu_pct,cpu_utilization,kernel_percent,cpumem_usage_cpu,""))
| eval mem_used_pct=tonumber(coalesce(mem_used_pct,mem_usage_pct,memory_usage,heap_used_pct,chassis_mem_pct,""))
| eval fib_used=tonumber(coalesce(fib_used,fib_entries_used,ipv4_routes_used,forwarding_entries_used,l3_route_entries,""))
| eval fib_max=tonumber(coalesce(fib_max,fib_entries_max,route_capacity_max,forwarding_entries_max,fib_total_entries,""))
| eval tcam_used=tonumber(coalesce(tcam_used,tcam_entries_used,hardware_tcam_used,""))
| eval tcam_max=tonumber(coalesce(tcam_max,tcam_entries_max,tcam_budget_total,""))
| eval tcam_pct_direct=tonumber(coalesce(tcam_util_pct,acl_tcam_used_pct,""))
| eval fib_util=if(isnotnull(fib_used) AND isnotnull(fib_max) AND fib_max>0, round(100*fib_used/fib_max,2), null())
| eval tcam_util=if(isnotnull(tcam_pct_direct), tcam_pct_direct, if(isnotnull(tcam_used) AND isnotnull(tcam_max) AND tcam_max>0, round(100*tcam_used/tcam_max,2), null()))
| eval syslog_res=if(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(tcam|fib|forwarding.?table|hw.?rate.?limit|low.?memory|out.?of.?memory|l2rib.*fail)"),1,0)
| eval bgp_state_l=lower(coalesce(bgp_state,state,""))
| eval bgp_churn=if(sourcetype="network:bgp_neighbor" AND match(bgp_state_l,"(idle|active|connect|opensent|openconfirm)"),1,0)
| eval cpu_b=if(isnotnull(cpu_pct) AND cpu_pct>=85,1,0)
| eval mem_b=if(isnotnull(mem_used_pct) AND mem_used_pct>=88,1,0)
| eval fib_b=if(isnotnull(fib_util) AND fib_util>=90,1,0)
| eval tcam_b=if(isnotnull(tcam_util) AND tcam_util>=85,1,0)
| eval health_sig=case(
    sourcetype="network:switch_health" AND match(lower(coalesce(health_status,status,resource_state,"")),"critical|major|down|failed"),4,
    true(),0)
| fillnull value=0 syslog_res bgp_churn cpu_b mem_b fib_b tcam_b health_sig
| eval score=cpu_b*3 + mem_b*3 + fib_b*5 + tcam_b*5 + syslog_res*4 + bgp_churn*2 + health_sig
| where score>=3 OR fib_b==1 OR tcam_b==1 OR health_sig>=4
| bin _time span=10m
| stats max(cpu_pct) AS cpu_peak max(mem_used_pct) AS mem_peak max(fib_util) AS fib_util_peak max(tcam_util) AS tcam_util_peak max(score) AS score_peak values(sourcetype) AS st_seen latest(fib_used) AS fib_used_latest latest(fib_max) AS fib_cap latest(tcam_used) AS tcam_used_latest latest(tcam_max) AS tcam_cap BY _time leaf
| eval story=leaf."|cpu=".tostring(round(coalesce(cpu_peak,0)))."|mem=".tostring(round(coalesce(mem_peak,0)))."|fib=".tostring(round(coalesce(fib_util_peak,0)))."|tcam=".tostring(round(coalesce(tcam_util_peak,0)))
| sort - score_peak - _time
| head 200
| table _time leaf cpu_peak mem_peak fib_util_peak tcam_util_peak fib_used_latest fib_cap tcam_used_latest tcam_cap score_peak st_seen story```

**Understanding this SPL** — The search unions structured polls, health scores, syslog hints, and BGP FSM without joins. **coalesce()** absorbs field renames across NX-OS maintenance releases. **fib_util** and **tcam_util** are comparable 0–100 scores from counts or percent feeds. **syslog_res** and **bgp_churn** add confidence when REST is throttled. **health_sig** honors external CRITICAL states when native polls lag during auth failures. **score** weights FIB/TCAM breaches above brief CPU spikes. 10m bins smooth sampling jitter while sustained faults repeat across bins.

**Pipeline walkthrough** — Scope indexes and five sourcetypes; normalize **leaf** keys; cast metrics with **tonumber**; derive utilization ratios safely; flag syslog and non-Established BGP; apply starter thresholds (CPU ≥85, mem ≥88, FIB ≥90, TCAM ≥85); aggregate by time bin and leaf; output **story** for compact pages.

Step 3 — Validate
• Compare **show system resources** CPU to **cpu_peak** and **show hardware capacity forwarding** columns to **fib_used_latest/fib_cap**; allow ≤2% rounding drift when banks are shared.
• On EOS, reconcile platform commands with **fib_util_peak**; ensure slot tagging matches CLI scope (MPU vs linecard).
• Inject a lab TCAM trap if permitted and confirm **syslog_res** flips within one window.
• Match BGP Established counts to **show bgp l2vpn evpn summary**; mismatches imply poll VRF/credential issues, not fabric loss.
• Run timechart span=15m count by sourcetype; post-upgrade flatlines usually mean modinput trust or SSL errors.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• Wallboard row: single-value **max(score_peak)** per site with red/amber/green thresholds.
• Analyst row: heatmap leaf × utilization metrics with per-site z-score coloring.
• ITSM row: table with **story**, **st_seen**, caps, timestamps—no secrets in exports.
• Drilldowns open **7777** reference dashboards using **hostname** tokens only.

Step 5 — Visualization, alerting, and troubleshooting
**Visualization** — Line charts for **cpu_peak** and **mem_peak** paired with area charts for **fib_util_peak** and **tcam_util_peak** over seven days; overlay change tickets. Add a histogram of **score_peak** by pod for capacity reviews.

**Alert design** — Every **10 minutes** over **earliest=-4h@m**; fire when **score_peak≥8** or **fib_util_peak≥90** for two consecutive runs; throttle **45 minutes** per leaf; honor **suppress_until** from **fabric_leaf_inventory**; payloads include **story**, **st_seen**, **fib_cap**, **tcam_cap**—never API passwords or private AS details.

**Troubleshooting** — High CPU with clean FIB suggests process storms—capture **show processes cpu** sorted data before RMA. Post-clear EVPN relearn spikes may self-heal—require sustained bins before sev-1. Null **nxos:hardware_capacity** after SUP failover often reflects RBAC path changes. Duplicate FQDN vs short-name hosts split bins—enforce canonical **hostname** in CMDB. Bad **tcam_util** on EOS usually means **vendor_capacity_map** drift—collect **show tech-support** for vendor support.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how hard each leaf works inside—busy brains, memory use, and how full the hardware route books are—so we fix strains before apps stumble or east-west traffic suffers.
hardware: Cisco Nexus 9000/3000 leaf switches (NX-OS); Arista 7050X3/7280R3-class leaves (EOS)
knownFalsePositives:
**planned_linecard_upgrade** — During ISSU or linecard replacements, `show hardware capacity` may report transient zeros or split totals across old and new ASICs while the supervisor reconciles banks. Splunk can show a fake **fib_util_peak** dip-then-spike pattern. Require two consecutive polls after declared maintenance complete, or honor `suppress_until` on **fabric_leaf_inventory** before paging capacity teams.

**bgp_refresh_after_policy** — Mass concurrent soft-inbound refreshes when controllers publish broad retract/announce churn can pin CPU high for minutes without any hardware table breach. Peaks align with `network:bgp_neighbor` state flutters but forwarding remains sound. Correlate with automation job IDs, damp CPU-only alerts during known publish windows, and require **fib_b** or **tcam_b** before hardware escalations.

**telemetry_alias_drift** — TA upgrades occasionally rename `cpu_utilization` or TCAM fields; `tonumber()` yields nulls so breaches vanish although the CLI still looks hot. Run field-summary dashboards after every TA push, keep **FIELDALIAS** stanzas for legacy sourcetypes, and alert on sudden null-rate increases—not only on numeric thresholds.

**cold_standby_supervisor** — On dual-sup chassis, standby polls may return idle CPU while active forwarding happens on the peer. Mis-tagging **hostname** without **active_role** can flatten **cpu_peak** misleadingly. Enrich events with **redundancy_state** when available and filter standbys from capacity tiles meant for traffic-forwarding units only.

**nms_false_critical** — Third-party **network:switch_health** probes sometimes mark CRITICAL when SNMP communities rotate or proxy ACLs block UDP, not when TCAM fills. Cross-check native **nxos:system_resources** before acting, and include probe collector_id in tickets so network and monitoring ownership stays clear.

**evpn_type5_bursts** — Sudden Type-5 advertisement storms expand RIB→FIB programming queues and inflate CPU without immediate TCAM exhaustion. **bgp_churn** may trip while **fib_util** is still modest. Treat as control-plane contention: widen investigation SPL with `show bgp l2vpn evpn summary` counters and throttle repeat alerts per **leaf** until prefixes stabilize.

**eos_bank_mapping_gap** — Arista platform releases occasionally rename TCAM bank labels; **vendor_capacity_map** rows go stale and **tcam_util** reads artificially low. Refresh the CSV after EOS upgrades, validate against one manual `show platform` snapshot per pod, and tag events with **eos_version** for quicker comparisons.
